content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 27 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 223–224 | **Source Images:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/DEU_027_page_001.jpg, DEU_027_page_002.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), p. 159 — DEU_027_vaticanus_page_001.jpg

---

**1** And Moshe commanded, together with the elders of Yisra'el, the people, saying: Keep all the commandment that I am commanding you today.

**2** And it shall be on the day you cross over the Yarden into the land that the LORD your God is giving you, you shall set up for yourself great stones and plaster them with lime.

**3** And you shall write upon them all the words of this Torah when you cross over — so that you may enter the land that the LORD your God is giving you, a land flowing with milk and honey, as the LORD, the God of your fathers, spoke to you.

**4** And it shall be, when you have crossed over the Yarden, you shall set up these stones on Mount 'Eval, as I am commanding you today, and you shall plaster them with lime.

**5** And you shall build there an altar to the LORD your God — an altar of stones; you shall not lift upon them any iron [tool].

**6** Of whole stones you shall build the altar of the LORD your God, and you shall offer upon it burnt offerings to the LORD your God.

**7** And you shall sacrifice peace offerings and eat there, and rejoice before the LORD your God.

**8** And you shall write upon the stones all the words of this Torah, very clearly.

**9** And Moshe and the Levitical priests spoke to all Yisra'el, saying: Be silent and listen, O Yisra'el: this day you have become a people for the LORD your God.

**10** And you shall listen to the voice of the LORD your God and do his commandments and his statutes that I am commanding you today.

**11** And Moshe commanded the people on that day, saying:

**12** These shall stand to bless the people on Mount Gerizim when you have crossed over the Yarden: Shim'on and Levi and Yehudah and Yissakhar and Yosef and Binyamin.

**13** And these shall stand for the curse on Mount 'Eval: Re'uven, Gad, and Asher, and Zevulun, Dan, and Naftali.

**14** And the Levites shall answer and say to every man of Yisra'el with a loud voice:

**15** Cursed [arur] is the man who makes a carved image or a cast metal image — an abomination to the LORD, the work of the hands of a craftsman — and sets [it] in secret. And all the people shall answer and say: Amen.

**16** Cursed [arur] is he who dishonors his father and his mother. And all the people shall say: Amen.

**17** Cursed [arur] is he who removes his neighbor's boundary marker. And all the people shall say: Amen.

**18** Cursed [arur] is he who causes the blind to wander from the road. And all the people shall say: Amen.

**19** Cursed [arur] is he who perverts the justice of the sojourner, the fatherless, and the widow. And all the people shall say: Amen.

**20** Cursed [arur] is he who lies with his father's wife, because he has uncovered the covering of his father. And all the people shall say: Amen.

**21** Cursed [arur] is he who lies with any beast. And all the people shall say: Amen.

**22** Cursed [arur] is he who lies with his sister — his father's daughter or his mother's daughter. And all the people shall say: Amen.

**23** Cursed [arur] is he who lies with his mother-in-law. And all the people shall say: Amen.

**24** Cursed [arur] is he who strikes his neighbor in secret. And all the people shall say: Amen.

**25** Cursed [arur] is he who takes a bribe to strike down an innocent person [nephesh]. And all the people shall say: Amen.

**26** Cursed [arur] is he who does not uphold the words of this Torah, to do them. And all the people shall say: Amen.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**V.4 — Mount 'Eval:** The MT clearly reads 'Eval (the cursed mountain), where the altar is to be built. The Samaritan Pentateuch reads "Mount Gerizim" here instead — a well-known sectarian variant reflecting Samaritan holy site ideology. LXX Vaticanus (p. 159) reads Γαιβαλ (Gaival) — supporting the MT against the Samaritan reading.

**V.8 — "Very clearly" (ba'er heitev):** Lit. "explain/inscribe it well." Some traditions understand this as meaning in 70 languages. LXX: σαφως σφοδρα — "very plainly/clearly."

**V.12–13 — The two mountains:** Gerizim for blessing; 'Eval for curse. The tribal division: Leah's sons (less Re'uven and Zevulun) + Rachel's sons on Gerizim; Re'uven, Gad (Leah's concubine-sons), Asher (Zilpah's son), Zevulun, Dan (Bilhah's son), Naftali on 'Eval. The groupings reflect birth-order and matrilineal lineage complexities.

**V.15–26 — Twelve curses (arur):** Each curse (arur) covers a sin committed in secret — where human courts cannot see. This suggests the dodecalogue of curses functions as a divine surveillance system. EMET preserves arur with translation "cursed" and notes the term in brackets. The LXX uses επικαταρατος throughout — a precise equivalent.

**V.15 — "Sets [it] in secret":** Hebrew ba-sater — hidden idolatry. The public curses govern private sins; the congregational "Amen" constitutes acceptance of divine jurisdiction over hidden acts.

**V.26 — "Uphold the words of this Torah":** LXX renders: ος ουκ εμμενει εν πασιν τοις λογοις — "who does not abide in all the words." Paul quotes this in Gal 3:10 in a theological argument about the law's scope.

— EMET
